highlights.text: I wrote way back on Friday August 1st at 4pm: “The bull case Monday is that ES can do some back-testing of the zones we broke down yesterday and today.
I wrote Thursday at 4pm (when were 6360s): “My general lean is to defer to the trend.

I got long at 420PM yesterday, stating in yesterdays newsletter at the same time : “I have added long exposure a little after the close ~6396 as we put in a nice Failed Breakdown of the 6396 low set at 2:40PM.” I also wrote: “My general lean though is always to defer to the trend.
One of those building blocks is my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Failed Breakdowns however do not occur in a vacuum.
They always require what I call an elevator down sell first, which is another building block.
This condition is then always followed by a Failed Breakdown, and subsequent squeeze higher.
Heading into August 1st, ES had seen the biggest “elevator down” sell since May, selling 230 points down to 6240.
There was the cycle: Elevator down, into Failed Breakdown, into squeeze.
We saw it again Thursday last week: ES saw an “elevator down” sell, reversing from 6420s down to 6333.
Sentiment was bearish here, but we were longing, and last Thursday at around 2:50PM ES got a quality Failed Breakdown of a shelf of lows at 6344.
This was one of those “elevator down” sells, so we all know what was coming this morning.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the quality Failed Breakdown we had last Thursday at 6344 which gave us the massive run into this weeks highs (this one was interesting because it occurred after a non-stop red day and when we get a bear market, you’ll need to know these).

post_title: SPX Has Arrived At ATHs. Can It Sustain Now For More Upside? August 13 Plan
